CSRF/XSRF With a Decoupled Frontend and Backend
CSRF is pretty common and usually solvable, but with a fully decoupled frontend and backend, the approach is different.
Software Versioning: What Alpha, Beta, and RC Actually Mean

Quick reference for the version-stage names you see everywhere — Pre-alpha, Alpha, Beta, Release Candidate (RC), and the Latest-release / Pre-release tags on GitHub. Covers when it's safe-ish to use each in real projects, and the difference between open source and closed source conventions (Microsoft's RC vs. Apple's "Golden Master").
Can I Use? A Five-Minute Guide to Checking CSS / HTML / JS Browser Support

Frontend devs use new CSS / HTML / JS features all the time — sometimes without realizing they're not supported in every browser. Can I Use is the go-to support-checking service. This post is a five-minute tour of using it — searching for features, reading the support matrix and color legend, the vendor-prefix tooling, and a quick aside on what CSS prefixes are and when they matter.
GitHub Pages "Build Failure" — the .nojekyll Fix for Hexo and Static Sites

Deployed Hexo (or any static site) to GitHub Pages and got "The page build failed for the master/main branch" email? Two common causes — either GitHub Pages is having an outage, or GitHub Pages is trying to compile your site as Jekyll. This post covers the .nojekyll workaround and how to make Hexo include hidden files.
VSCode Settings I Recommend for Developers

A sharper tool means better work. After spending years in VSCode, I've put together the settings that make day-to-day dev work smoother — covering whitespace rendering, line endings, fonts, Emmet, and more.
git push Error GH007: Your push would publish a private email address.
I recently tweaked a GitHub setting and started getting `GH007: Your push would publish a private email address.` when pushing. Here's why it happens and how to fix it.
JavaScript Core (55) — Locking Objects Down: preventExtensions, seal, and freeze

Last time we looked at Object.defineProperty, which tweaks individual properties on an object. This time we go one level up and operate on the whole object — preventExtensions, seal, and freeze. I'll walk through what each one locks down, how they relate to each other, and why nested objects don't get frozen for free.
JavaScript Core (54) - Property Descriptors: The Hidden Settings on Every Object Property

Every property on an object carries a set of hidden settings called descriptors — whether it can be rewritten, deleted, or looped over. Once you get how these work, a lot of framework behavior suddenly makes a lot more sense. This post walks through configurable, enumerable, value, and writable, plus how to read and tweak them with Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor and Object.defineProperty.
What Is ESM (ES6 Modules)? And Why JavaScript Needed Modules in the First Place

What exactly is ESM, and how is it different from CommonJS? This post starts with why early JavaScript needed modules at all, then walks through the import/export syntax of ES Modules, plus how browser and Node.js support differ — with real examples along the way.
